data.title: Nansen CEO Clarifies Misconceptions About WLFI Transactions
data.description: According to PANews, Nansen's CEO, Alex Svanevik, addressed concerns on the X platform regarding WLFI transactions. Initially, an AI Research Agent suggested that Justin Sun was involved in selling WLFI. However, upon further examination of the transaction timestamps, it was determined that Sun did not engage in a sell-off. Svanevik also noted that the related transfers occurred after a significant drop in WLFI's value, indicating they were not the primary cause of the price decline. Furthermore, based on the day's overall trading volume, these transactions likely did not have a substantial impact on the market price.

data.title: The total amount of XTTA destroyed reached 40 million, accounting for 10% of the total issuance
data.description: According to official information, TrendX has destroyed a total of 40,000,000 XTTA since the launch of its hashrate mining mechanism, representing 10% of the total supply. Each destruction increases XTTA's scarcity and further solidifies its long-term value proposition. TrendX stated that with the continued expansion of the hashrate market and the continued development of its ecosystem, XTTA will gradually enter a more deflationary phase. Notably, the company predicts that with the continued growth of the hashrate market, the destruction of XTTA is expected to exceed 50% of the total supply, ushering in a period of strong deflation.

data.title: Galaxy Digital pledged 387,000 HYPE, worth approximately $18.2 million
data.description: Galaxy Digital staked 387,000 HYPE tokens, worth approximately $18.2 million, through its private validator 17 hours ago. Previously, these tokens had been stored in Galaxy Digital's HyperEVM address but had not been staked.

data.title: Ether.fi foundation repurchases $314K in ETHFI burns 155K tokens
data.description:
6-9-2025 – The Ether. fi Foundation has used 73 ETH in protocol revenue, worth about $314,000, to repurchase 264,000 ETHFI tokens this week. Announced by the foundation on X, the buyback saw 155,000 ETHFI tokens burned to reduce circulating supply, while the remaining 108,000 were distributed to sETHFI holders—stakers of the governance token. source: https://www.cointrackdaily.com/ether-fi-foundation-repurchases-314k-in-ethfi-burns-155k-tokens/

data.title: 2 cryptocurrencies to reach a $200 billion market cap in 2026
data.description:
Although the cryptocurrency market remains volatile, the sector is still viewed as bullish, with expectations of continued capital inflows in the coming months. Several digital assets are well-positioned to reach record levels, including potentially achieving a market capitalization of $200 billion by 2026.  To this end, Finbold has identified the following assets with the strongest potential to hit this milestone. XRP XRP has been one of the best-performing assets in recent months and is edging closer to the $200 billion market cap threshold
source: https://finbold.com/2-cryptocurrencies-to-reach-a-200-billion-market-cap-in-2026/

data.title: Cryptocurrency Market Experiences Significant Liquidations
data.description: According to Foresight News, data from Coinglass reveals that the cryptocurrency market saw liquidations totaling $270 million over the past 24 hours. Long positions accounted for $181 million, while short positions amounted to $89.06 million. Ethereum experienced liquidations of $83.48 million, and Bitcoin saw $89.35 million in liquidations.
